Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The Experimental procedures (BiFC and Co-IP assay) explicitly list PFP1a1 as LOC_Os02g48360.

Source_Species.reason: The study is in rice, and PFP1a1 is presented as a rice gene.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper concerns rice; Oryza sativa is directly inferable from the context of rice genetics and cited locus IDs.

Applied_Species.reason: The applied species throughout the study is rice.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Applied species is rice, i.e. Oryza sativa.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The mutant background used is the japonica rice cultivar Hwacheong.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper shows PFP1a1 interacts with PFP1b and is part of the proposed heterotetramer, but PFP1a1 itself was not genetically perturbed, so its role in 'supporting starch biosynthesis-related carbon metabolism' is mechanistic inference rather than directly tested phenotype.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The mechanistic context is regulation of starch biosynthesis/endosperm starch metabolism.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper treats starch as the major seed storage carbohydrate affecting seed quality.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: All starch phenotypes discussed are in rice seed endosperm.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper states expression of PFP1a1 was significantly increased in the pfp1-3 mutant (Figure S3).

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Cytosolic localization was directly shown for PFP1/PFP1b and BiFC signals for PFP1a1/PFP1a2 were in the cytoplasm, but PFP1a1 alone was not separately shown as a localization assay in the main results.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: PFP1a1 was examined by BiFC, Co-IP, and qRT-PCR/Figure S3.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point matches the results: interaction with PFP1b, self/other dimerization, and increased transcript level in pfp1-3.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper describes PFP1a proteins as putative alpha-subunits of PFP1.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Results state PFP1a1 interacts with PFP1b and BiFC shows PFP1a1 forms homodimers and heterodimers with other subunits including PFP1a2.

Other_Important_Info.reason: This accurately reflects that PFP1a proteins were analyzed for interaction/complex formation, not mapped as causal mutant genes.

Omics_Data.reason: qRT-PCR, BiFC and Co-IP are supported, and RNA-seq context exists in the paper, but RNA-seq was not specifically presented as core data for PFP1a1; 'accession NA' is not discussed.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Interaction assays were conducted by transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a forward-looking application claim not directly demonstrated in the Results.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper supports interactions of PFP1a2 with PFP1b and self/other subunits including PFP1a1.

Other_Important_Info.reason: This accurately summarizes that PFP1a2 is one of three alpha-subunits able to bind PFP1b and was not independently shown as the causal gene.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper links starch with endosperm quality and grain filling.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The title and text describe PFP1/PFP1b as pyrophosphate-fructose 6-phosphate 1-phosphotransferase beta subunit.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number 2.7.1.90 is given anywhere in the paper.

Source_Species.reason: The gene studied is from rice.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The mutant was isolated from japonica cv. Hwacheong.

Applied_Species.reason: The experiments were performed in rice.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species is Oryza sativa.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The applied rice variety is Hwacheong for the mutant/wild-type comparison.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: All listed effects are directly reported in Results: floury endosperm, slower grain filling, reduced starch and grain weight, increased protein and lipid.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The Summary states PFP1 reversibly converts fructose 6-phosphate and pyrophosphate.

Primary_Product.reason: The Summary states conversion to fructose 1,6-bisphosphate and orthophosphate.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This reaction description is explicitly stated in the Summary/Discussion.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The paper directly links PFP1b to starch biosynthesis and carbohydrate/glycolytic metabolism.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The text describes PFP1 as a glycolytic enzyme affecting carbon flux toward endosperm starch biosynthesis.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: PFP1 acts upstream of starch accumulation by controlling glycolytic intermediates and carbon flux.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The paper supports an indirect connection through precursor/carbon flux supply to starch biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The terminal product directly measured and affected is starch.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper links starch to grain weight, endosperm texture, seed quality and germination-related seed vigor.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The effects are in rice seed endosperm.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: RT-PCR showed expression in root, stem and leaf, and reduced transcript in pfp1-3; developing endosperm involvement is supported by seed phenotypes and seed transcript analyses.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper reports expression in root, stem, leaf, and also analyzes developing seeds/endosperm by qRT-PCR/RNA-seq.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The Discussion cites confocal analysis showing PFP-GFP signals solely in the cytosol (Figure S4).

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are directly used in the paper for PFP1b characterization.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each numbered claim is directly supported by Results figures/text, including 6.3% activity, complementation, starch/lipid/protein effects, embryo/germination defects, and omics/metabolite changes.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq at 3, 6 and 12 DAP and metabolomics methods are supported, but 'accession NA' is not stated in the paper.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: These conditions are explicitly described: natural paddy field, developing seeds at 3/6/12 DAP, and germination on half-strength MS without sugar.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper describes it as the PFP beta-subunit and pyrophosphate-fructose 6-phosphate 1-phosphotransferase family member.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: BiFC/Co-IP support interactions with PFP1a1, PFP1a2, and PFP1a4; BiFC also showed PFP1b self-interaction.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The Discussion explicitly calls the PFP reaction a rate-limiting step in regulation of primary carbohydrate metabolic flux.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: All stated tradeoffs are directly observed in the mutant Results.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper suggests utility for breeding higher grain yield and quality, but specific engineering strategies such as gene stacking are not tested.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly describes PFP1b as the regulatory beta-subunit and reports effects on lipid metabolites and T6P/glycolytic intermediates.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Complementation used the maize ubiquitin promoter to drive PFP1 coding sequence.

corrections.reason: No EC number 2.7.1.90 is given anywhere in the paper.
